additional_information: The blast furnace slag input has been economically allocated as a co-product of pig iron production.
The impact of the siliceous fly ash (a waste from coal-fire electricity production) has been allocated to the previous system.

The B-PCR provides default transport scenarios for the transport to the building site for cases where specific data on transport are missing. The B-PCR provides scenarios for this life cycle stage. The following transport steps apply: – 75% directly to the construction site over 100 km with a Lorry 16-32 ton (EURO5) (ecoinvent record: 'Transport, freight, lorry 16-32 metric ton, EURO5 {RER}| transport, freight, lorry 16-32 metric ton, EURO5 | Cut-off, U') – 25% to a supplier over 100 km with a >32 ton lorry (ecoinvent record: 'Transport, freight, lorry >32 metric ton, EURO5 {RER}| transport, freight, lorry >32 metric ton, EURO5 | Cut-off, U'). Afterwards the product is transported from the supplier to the construction site. 90% of the 25% is transported with a Lorry 16-32 ton (EURO5) over a distance of 35km and 10% of the 25% is transported with a Lorry 7.5-16 ton (EURO5) over a distance of 35km. (ecoinvent records: 'Transport, freight, lorry 16-32 metric ton, EURO5 {RER}| transport, freight, lorry 16-32 metric ton, EURO5 | Cut-off, U', 'Transport, freight, lorry 7.5-16 metric ton, EURO5 {RER}| transport, freight, lorry 7.5-16 metric ton, EURO5 | Cut-off, U')
